page: Amazon Web Services c7i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 105 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores both rank in the top 10% of tested systems. The DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier read-only uncached bandwidth of 419.18 GB/sec and cached bandwidth of 13859.76 GB/sec. Cryptographic operations and extended instruction tests also register in the top 10% due to hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instructions. For database and web workloads, the instance demonstrates high efficiency, achieving 176.17 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 115.71 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c7i.48xlarge offers high compute density and memory throughput for workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware. The inclusion of Intel AMX allows the instance to achieve strong performance in CPU-based machine learning tasks, such as LLM inference, where it processes 232.86 tokens per second on medium 7B models without requiring expensive GPU resources. This makes the instance highly suitable for compute-heavy workloads, including high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale software compilation, intensive cryptographic processing, Redis-based caching layers, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Operating at 3.2 GHz, this x86_64 platform features 384.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, with no local storage or GPUs. It delivers top-tier performance, ranking in the top 10% for multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations. Hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX enhance its capability for vector math and CPU-based matrix operations. With a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is highly efficient for high-throughput workloads. It is optimized for compute-intensive tasks, including large-scale software compilation, high-performance web serving, Redis caching, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized platform featuring advanced Intel matrix extensions and top-tier multi-core processing power for demanding enterprise workloads.

384 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations.

Best suited for high-throughput web serving, Redis caching, and CPU-based LLM inference.
